name: Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife — circular desde Granada
region_intro: Hiking in Parque Periurbano Dehesa Del Generalife offers diverse landscapes adjacent to Granada, characterized by Mediterranean flora including olive groves, pine forests, and traditional dehesa. The region features the Darro River valley and varied elevations, providing panoramic views of the city, the Alhambra, and the Sierra Nevada mountains. Historical elements such as ancient acequias and Moorish ruins are integrated into the natural setting. Best hiking trails in Parque Periurbano Dehesa Del Generalife The most popular hiking route is Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife — circular desde Granada, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views of Granada and the Alhambra. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sacromonte — circular route, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This trail winds through the historic Sacromonte area, providing views of its unique cave dwellings and the Darro valley. Local hikers also love the Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife – circular desde Casas del Hornillo, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail leading through varied Mediterranean woodlands and historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Hiking in Parque Periurbano Dehesa Del Generalife is defined by Mediterranean forests, river valleys, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Parque Periurbano Dehesa Del Generalife are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 3,700 reviews. More than 18,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Parque Periurbano Dehesa Del Generalife's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: There are nearly 500 hiking routes in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. This includes 191 easy, 238 moderate, and 57 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trails in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: The park features diverse Mediterranean landscapes, including olive groves, reforested pine forests, and traditional dehesa. You'll encounter varied elevations, with some trails following the Darro River valley and others ascending to offer panoramic views. The terrain can range from gentle paths to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: Yes, the park offers numerous family-friendly trails. Many of the 191 easy routes are suitable for families. These paths often wind through pleasant woodlands and offer opportunities to enjoy nature without strenuous climbs. Consider routes that explore the recreational areas like Llano de la Perdiz.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the trails in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife is generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the trails with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I discover while hiking?
A: The park is rich in history. You can explore remnants of its Moorish past, including ancient &lt;em&gt;acequias&lt;/em&gt; (water channels) and the ruins of the Palace of Dar Al-Arusa. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rainwater cistern (Aljibe de la Lluvia)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3034220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jesús del Valle Estate&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1029583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacromonte Abbey&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant nearby attraction.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the park?
A: Yes, many trails in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984064575/parque-periurbano-dehesa-del-generalife-circular-desde-casas-del-hornillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife – circular desde Casas del Hornillo&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 2 hours 41 minutes to complete.

Q: What are the best viewpoints in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: Hikers are rewarded with spectacular panoramic views throughout the park. The Mirador de la Silla del Moro (Silla del Moro Viewpoint) is particularly renowned for its stunning vistas of the Alhambra, the city of Granada, and the Sierra Nevada mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2641102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Sol Summit&lt;/a&gt; also offers excellent perspectives.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: Hike durations vary significantly depending on the trail's length and difficulty. Easy routes might take an hour or two, while moderate trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351648716/sacromonte-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacromonte — circular route&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 2 hours 19 minutes. Longer or more challenging routes can extend to several hours.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife. The weather is mild, and the vegetation is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers cooler temperatures and potentially snow-capped views of the Sierra Nevada.

Q: Is there public transport access to Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: Yes, due to its proximity to Granada, the park is easily accessible by public transport. Local bus services from Granada can take you to various access points around the park, making it convenient for hikers without private vehicles.

Q: Where can I park when visiting Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: There are several parking areas available near the main entrances and recreational zones of the park, such as around Llano de la Perdiz. Given its urban proximity, it's advisable to check specific access points for designated parking facilities, especially during peak seasons.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in the park?
A: The diverse woodlands of Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife are home to a rich array of animal species, particularly various woodland birds. The park boasts over 111 cataloged animal species, so keep an eye out for local fauna during your hike.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Generalife?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 3,700 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning panoramic views of Granada and the Alhambra, the diverse Mediterranean landscapes, and the historical elements integrated into the trails.
tips: Section of the royal ditch that supplies the Alhambra with water.

It is located at an altitude of 1013 meters, so the temperature here is cooler than in the city, a good reason to go up on a summer Sunday. This makes the views spectacular, on one side, the mountains of Granada and on the other the city with the Alhambra in the foreground.

waypoints.name: View of the Alhambra, the gardens, and Granada
